text: Initially, liver biopsy was performed to evaluate fatty liver and other pathologies. Laparoscopic cholecystectomy was done.
The surgeon uses ultrasonic shears to mobilize the right liver, followed by dissection and ligation of the short hepatic vein with a Hem-o-lock clip.

The surgeon meticulously dissects and isolates the common bile duct, right hepatic artery, and portal vein using vessel loops. Temporary clamping of the right portal vein and right hepatic artery is performed, followed by marking the demarcation line on the liver surface with electric cautery.
The surgeon ligates the middle hepatic vein branches for segments V and VIII, preparing these anterior section branches for later reconstruction.
Following the completion of parenchymal transection, the surgeon divides the right hepatic duct, portal vein, and hepatic artery, separating these structures from the liver tissue.
The right liver graft is extracted through a suprapubic transverse incision. Fibrin glue is applied to the cut surface of the liver, and a drain is inserted to complete the operation.
